semester_end_exam.note: Two third weightage for final reckoning i.e., 50 marks

objectives: To understand different paradigms of algorithms such as greedy, dynamic programming, divide and conquer etc.
To calculate the time complexities of algorithms.
The ability to decide based on a given problem which design paradigm and algorithm is appropriate.

modules.title: Models of computation & Algorithm design frameworks
topics: Algorithm design frameworks - Divide/Decrease and Conquer, Backtracking, Greedy, Dynamic Programming
Comparison - Divide & Conquer, Greedy and Dynamic Programming

modules.title: Sorting
topics: Comparison based sorts - Bubble sort, insertion sort, selection sort, quick sort, merge sort; analysis and comparison
Non-comparison based sorts - Radix sort, count sort

modules.title: Illustrations of various design framework
topics: Dynamic Programming - Optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems; Matrix-chain multiplication
Greedy Method - Knapsack problem, Job sequencing with deadlines

modules.title: Graph Algorithms
topics: BFS and DFS - algorithm and comparison
Prim's and Kruskal's algorithms for finding minimum spanning tree

modules.title: P and NP
topics: Notion of NP Class: P, NP, NP-hard, NP-complete

textbooks_and_references.title: Modern Electronic Communication, Principles and Practice
textbooks_and_references.note: Appears unrelated to the algorithm course; listed in provided excerpt header.

notes: Structured directly from the provided Design.pdf excerpt. No explicit lab/tutorial components; theory-only course with 3 credits.
